formations_blocks: Panama possession-based build-up with short passing
Ghana deep, reactive defending in their own half
Ghana occasional forward-ball/long-ball outlet for Semenyo
key_matchups: Nuamah vs his marker on the right
phases.summary: Panama controlled the early phase with a mix of short possession and direct balls, pinning Ghana largely in their own half and creating the best chances through crosses and forward service. Ghana’s attacking answers were sporadic and mostly direct, with an early punt toward Semenyo and a later right-side break for Nuamah, but they struggled to sustain pressure or generate anything clear.

formations_blocks: Panama controlled possession through patient circulation and build-up
Ghana used an increasingly aggressive press before settling into a more front-foot phase
Panama were forced deeper into a defensive block as the half progressed
adjustments: Ghana's pressing intensity increased after the hydration break
Ghana resorted to quicker fouls when Panama tried to break forward
Panama's forward movement diminished and they shifted toward safer passing
Ghana won territory via a dangerous free kick after sustained pressure
key_matchups: Martinez vs the Ghana back line
Ati-Zigi vs Panamanian attackers in the penalty area
phases.summary: Panama began the phase with the better of possession, using a measured passing game that kept Ghana pinned back, but their attacks became less progressive as time wore on. After the hydration break, Ghana’s press sharpened and they forced Panama deeper, generating turnovers, a dangerous free kick, and a more territorial spell of pressure. The phase also featured several physical duels and penalty-area incidents, with Cordoba prominent in Panama’s build-up and Ati-Zigi repeatedly involved under aerial pressure.

formations_blocks: Panama attack with two players wide, Waterman central, and two midfielders close to the penalty box
adjustments: Ghana need to sort out their sputtering attack at halftime
Lawrence Ati-Zigi was replaced at halftime by Benjamin Asare
key_matchups: Lawrence Ati-Zigi vs Panama's early chances and crosses
Brandon Thomas-Asante vs Panama's defensive setup
phases.summary: Panama looked the more organized side, with a clear attacking pattern built around width, a central striker, and midfield runners arriving near the box. Ghana’s attack was hard to define from the commentary and lacked coherence, while their halftime goalkeeper change came after Ati-Zigi had already influenced the game with an early save and several cross claims.

formations_blocks: Panama back-and-forth possession with long balls
adjustments: Ghana replace injured Ati-Zigi with Asare at goalkeeper
Ghana introduce Fatawu and Thomas-Asante for Sulemana and Nuamah, adding more attacking potency
Panama continue to look long and lose sharpness in passing
Panama make double substitution at 63' with Waterman and Martinez off
key_matchups: Adjetey vs Mosquera on the set-piece header
Rodriguez crowded by four yellow shirts on the left flank
phases.summary: This phase is defined by a stop-start tactical shift after halftime, with Ghana losing Ati-Zigi to injury but becoming slightly more dangerous in attack and more direct from set pieces and long balls. Panama struggle to sustain first-half sharpness, often recycling possession or going long, while both sides trade midfield duels and half-chances rather than creating sustained pressure.

formations_blocks: Panama abandoned the press and dropped into a less aggressive defensive approach
Ghana circulated possession deep in Panama's half against a retreating block
Panama's defense became more aggressive at blocking passes and disrupting play
adjustments: Panama substituted Blackman for Godoy late on
Ghana continued to attack despite losing possession quickly from throw-ins
key_matchups: Murillo squaring up with a defender
Diaz shooting from an awkward position with Asare collecting
Thomas-Asante's late shot that was ultimately ruled out by the flag
phases.summary: This phase was dominated by fatigue, poor passing, and a shift away from Panama’s press, allowing Ghana to control the ball higher up the pitch. Panama still threatened sporadically on counters, but their final ball and combinations repeatedly broke down, while Ghana’s own attacks were frequently disrupted by aggressive defending. The game stayed tense and scrappy until the late breakthrough, when Ghana finally scored in stoppage time through Yirenkyi after a best chance had been ruled out for offside.
